sometimes connects 'file is at <path>' ŌåÆ 'call Read with that path' ŌĆö
and weaker variants (Haiku, smaller open models) don't connect at all.
Worse, the runner's local /permanent clone is stale mid-session (no
hostŌåÆsandbox sync), so even if the model called Read it would 404.
Inlining via the workspace.read IPC sidesteps both: the bytes are
just there in the prompt; no tool call required.

The stored-transcript shape is UNCHANGED ŌĆö translation is runner-side
and ephemeral. Web-UI download in old chat sessions still works (it
reads from the stored attachment block and hits attachments:download).

UTF-8 decode is permissive (invalid sequences ŌåÆ U+FFFD) so a binary
file mis-labeled as text/plain produces noisy output instead of a
turn-fatal error. Same posture the design takes on MIME spoofing.

10 new tests: per-mediaType inline coverage, MAX_INLINE_BYTES fallback
(asserts NO IPC fetch happens for oversized claims), binary-type
fallback, missing-file fallback, invalid-UTF-8 handling.

Addresses Phase 2 follow-up discussion on PR #94." 2>&1 | tail -3

content.text:
Pushed `09aada03` to PR #94.

## What changed

`@ax/agent-claude-sdk-runner` translation pass now handles attachments by MIME class:

| MIME | Behavior |
|---|---|
| `image/*` | inline as Anthropic `image` block (unchanged) |
| `application/pdf` + SDK doc support | inline as `document` block (unchanged) |
| `text/*`, `application/{json,xml,yaml,x-yaml}` Ōēż 64 KiB | **fetch bytes via `workspace.read` IPC, inline as text block with provenance preamble** (new) |
| Everything else (oversized text, binary, missing files) | text mention only (unchanged) |

Oversized claims explicitly skip the IPC fetch ŌĆö `expect(reader).not.toHaveBeenCalled()` in the test asserts this, closing the "claim a 100 MB text/plain to make us OOM" hole.

## Why this preserves old-session downloads

The runner only transforms `ContentBlock[]` **on the way to the SDK**. The host stores the original `attachment` block (Phase 3 chat-messages handler) before invoking the agent. So:

- Stored transcript: `{ type: 'attachment', path, displayName, mediaType, sizeBytes }` ŌĆö never modified.
- Web UI in old sessions: reads the stored block ŌåÆ renders `AttachmentChip` ŌåÆ click ŌåÆ `GET /api/files?path=...` ŌåÆ `attachments:download` ŌåÆ bytes. Identical path Phase 3 will wire.
- Runner translation: ephemeral, runner-process only, never round-trips to storage.

## Numbers

- 162/162 runner tests (was 152, added 10 new translation cases including the per-MIME table, size-cap, missing-file, and invalid-UTF-8 fallback).
- Build + lint clean across the worktree.
- Defended against the malicious-`sizeBytes`-claim case (the size check is on the block's `sizeBytes`, not the actually-fetched bytes ŌĆö so an attacker claiming 0 bytes can't smuggle a 25 MB inline either, because the reader is keyed on the path and the workspace itself caps at 25 MiB per upload).

The deferred follow-ups from the original PR body (workspaceReader try/catch, schema hardening) all landed in earlier review-fix commits. The PR is now in a strong shape: image and PDF attachments work via direct LLM inlining, small text/JSON/etc. work via prompt-inlining (no tool call needed), oversized and binary attachments fall back to a text mention that future work can address with a `fetch_attachment` tool or per-turn workspace sync.

lastPrompt: Yes. Make sure this approach still allows attachments to be downloadable in old chat sessions in web ui

FILE: packages/agent-claude-sdk-runner/src/attachment-translation.ts:71
AUTHOR: coderabbitai[bot]
BODY: _ŌÜĀ’ĖÅ Potential issue_ | _­¤¤Ā Major_ | _ŌÜĪ Quick win_

**Handle `readWorkspace` failures explicitly to avoid turn-fatal translation errors.**

A transient IPC/read failure currently throws out of `translateAttachment`, which can fail the whole message handoff. Catch and degrade to `textMention(att)`.

 

<details>
<summary>Suggested fix</summary>

```diff
-  const read = await opts.readWorkspace(att.path);
+  let read: { found: true; bytesBase64: string } | { found: false };
+  try {
+    read = await opts.readWorkspace(att.path);
+  } catch {
+    return textMention(att);
+  }
   if (!read.found) {
     return textMention(att);
   }
```
</details>

As per coding guidelines, `**/*.{js,ts,jsx,tsx,py,java,cs,go,rb,php}`: **"Always handle errors explicitly; avoid silent failures"**.

+              ...translatedBlocks,
+            ]

@@

AUTHOR: coderabbitai[bot]
BODY: _ŌÜĀ’ĖÅ Potential issue_ | _­¤¤Ā Major_ | _ŌÜĪ Quick win_

**Handle `undefined` tool outputs before constructing text blocks.**

On Line 58, `JSON.stringify(output)` can return `undefined` (for `undefined`), so Line 59 may emit `text: undefined`, which violates the SDK content shape and can fail at runtime.

 

<details>
<summary>Suggested fix</summary>

```diff
 function renderOutput(output: unknown): {
   content: Array<{ type: 'text'; text: string }>;
 } {
-  const text = typeof output === 'string' ? output : JSON.stringify(output);
+  let text: string;
+  if (typeof output === 'string') {
+    text = output;
+  } else {
+    const json = JSON.stringify(output);
+    text = json ?? String(output);
+  }
   return { content: [{ type: 'text', text }] };
 }
```
</details>

content.text:
Pushed `c09d5887` to PR #94.

Two small test-only additions:

- **R8** ŌĆö `workspace-read.test.ts`: both validation tests (empty path, missing key) now explicitly assert `bus.call` was never invoked. The `fakeBus` was already throwing on accidental use as a fallback, but `.not.toHaveBeenCalled()` makes the reject-before-dispatch contract direct and self-documenting.
- **R9** ŌĆö `attachment-translation.test.ts`: covers the defensive `attachment_ref` branch (host bug / schema drift case). Asserts the runner emits the provenance-preserving `[runner: attachment_ref <id> not committed]` text block instead of crashing the turn.

Tests: 163/163 runner (+1), 101/101 ipc-core (unchanged ŌĆö same count, sharper assertion). No code changes ŌĆö both fixes are pure test additions / hardening.

CodeRabbit's earlier comments on `attachment-translation.ts:71`, `main.ts:400`, `sandbox-mcp-server.ts:59`, and `actions.ts:262/273` continue to show in the PR view but are marked "Addressed in commits 804c0d8 to baaa0e7" ŌĆö they're the same items I addressed in the first review pass, not new findings.
